title: mentha affinis
intro: Mentha affinis, also known as East Indian mint, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, closely related to peppermint and spearmint. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, due to its cooling and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Mentha affinis include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, relieve nausea and headaches, and support oral health by reducing plaque and bad breath. Herbal preparations of Mentha affinis include teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or used topically for their therapeutic benefits.
